exercises.name: Plate Weighted Wide Grip Pull Up
exercises.description:
1) Strap a weighted dip belt around your waist and grasp a pull up bar. 

2) Start by pulling your body up until your chin passes the bar. 

3) Pause, and lower your body until your arms are extended. 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Plate-Loaded Incline Machine Seated Chest Press
exercises.description: Sit with your back against the pad and grip the handles. Press upward and outward until your arms are extended. Slowly return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Plyometric Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on a mat with your legs straight, feet together, hands on the floor.

2.Push yourself up through elbow extension as quickly as possible, up until your hands leave the ground.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Pogo Hops
exercises.description: Perform small, quick jumps, staying on the balls of your feet and using minimal knee bend.

exercises.name: Pop Squats
exercises.description:
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and core engaged. Squat down explosively, then immediately jump upward, extending fully through the hips, knees, and ankles. 

Land softly and immediately return into the squat position to repeat the movement continuously. 

Maintain an upright torso and controlled landing to reduce impact.

exercises.name: Power Plate Plank
exercises.description: Power Plate Plank

exercises.name: Power squat
exercises.description: If you do not have this squat machine, you can use a barbell or smith machine to perform a squat. Put two small plates under your heels to elevate them.

exercises.name: Prayer Squat
exercises.description: Drop into a deep squat with hands in a prayer position at your chest. Use your elbows to gently push your knees outward for a stretch

exercises.name: Prisoner Squat
exercises.description:
1) Interlace your fingers behind your head.

2) Sit back and down by bending at the hips, knees, and ankles. 

3) Once you've reached the deepest position you can comfortably go, return back to a standing position by driving your feet into the floor.  

4) Repeat

exercises.name: Prisoner Squat to Jump
exercises.description:
Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands placed behind your head, elbows pointing out to the sides in a prisoner position. 

Engage your core and lower your body into a deep squat, keeping your chest upright and your knees tracking over your toes. From the bottom of the squat, explosively jump upward as high as possible while keeping your hands behind your head. 

Land softly with bent knees and immediately descend into the next squat to repeat the movement.

exercises.name: Prone Arm Swimmers
exercises.description: Lie face down and extend arms overhead. Alternate lifting your arms and legs in a swimming motion.

exercises.name: Prone Back Extension T's
exercises.description:
1. Lie face down on a mat, with your hands directly out to your sides, forming a letter "T"

2. Rotate your palms forward, so your thumbs are pointing directly upwards. 

3. Squeeze your shoulder blades together, while lifting your chest up off the ground. 

4. Flex your glutes, and lift your heels up off the ground while keeping your knees straight. 

5. Repeat for desired number of repetitions, or hold for desired time.

exercises.name: Prone Back Extension with Retraction
exercises.description:
1. Lie face down on a mat, with your arms out to your sides.

2. Rotate your hands so your palms face down towards your feet, and thumbs are pointed at the floor. 

3. Squeeze your shoulder blades together while lifting your chest up off the mat. 

4. Drive your shoulder blades down towards your back pockets, and hold this position for desired time.

exercises.name: Prone Chest Stretch
exercises.description: Prone Chest Stretch

exercises.name: Prone Chin Tuck (off edge of table)
exercises.description: Prone Chin Tuck (off edge of table)

exercises.name: Prone hammy curl machine glute emphasis
exercises.description: At the top part of the movement you will slightly pull glutes up to activate

exercises.name: Prone Neck Extensions
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on your chest, with your head supported by your hands. 

2. Gently flatten out the back of your head by tucking your chin. 

3. Lift your head up about an inch off the mat and hold that position.

exercises.name: Prone Scorpion
exercises.description:
1. When performing this mobility exercise, you'll want to ensure that you keep your shoulder press against the ground and that you rotate lightly and slowly through your back.

2. If you feel any pain in to your back while performing this exercise, stop and seek a professional healthcare provider (physiotherapist/rehabilitation specialist).

3. Lie on your stomach arms spread out like a T.

4. Look towards your right hand bringing your left cheek onto the floor.

5. Bend your right knee, point your right toe.

6. Lift that knee up the floor and just let the hips start to open.

7. Then back to neutral as your knee touch the floor.

8. Once you're done one side, repeat on the other.

exercises.name: Prone Scorpion Alternating
exercises.description:
Lie face down with arms extended out. Lift one leg, bend the knee, and rotate it across your body to touch the floor. 

Return to start and switch sides. Engage your glutes and hips.

exercises.name: Prone Scorpion Variation Alternating
exercises.description: Begin in a prone position. Lift one leg and rotate it across the body, but this time, extend the arm on the same side as the leg being lifted. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Pull Up
exercises.description:
1.Place your hands shoulder width apart and grab the pull up bar. Palms facing away from you, arms and legs straightened making your body hang.

2.Pull your body upwards until your chin is just over the bar.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Pulse Lateral Lunge
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding your hands at chest level or by your sides. 

Step one leg out to the side into a lateral lunge, keeping the opposite leg straight and your hips pushed back. From the bottom of the lunge, perform small pulses by moving up and down slightly without fully standing back up. 

Keep your chest upright, core engaged, and weight mostly on the bent leg.

exercises.name: Pulse Squat to Curtsy Pulse
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Lower into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back. 

Perform small pulses in the squat, moving slightly up and down without fully standing. From the squat, step one leg diagonally behind the other into a curtsy lunge and perform small pulses there as well. 

Keep your chest upright, core engaged, and weight mostly on the front leg. Return to the squat position and repeat.

exercises.name: Pulse Sumo Squats
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out. 

Lower your body into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back. From the bottom of the squat, perform small pulses by moving up and down slightly without fully standing up. 

Keep your chest upright, core engaged, and weight evenly distributed through your heels.

exercises.name: Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Lie with your chest facing down, palms flat on the floor and slightly more than shoulder-width apart, your feet should be together and your legs straight.

2.Push your body upwards and straighten your arms.

3.Lower your body returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Push Up
exercises.description: Perform a push-up with a wider hand placement, lowering the chest to the ground and then pressing back up.

exercises.name: Push Up to Cross Body Mountain Climbers
exercises.description:
Start in a high plank position with your hands under your shoulders and body in a straight line from head to heels. 

Perform one push-up. Immediately follow with a round of alternating cross-body mountain climbers, driving each knee toward the opposite elbow while keeping your hips low and core engaged. 

After completing the mountain climbers, return to plank and repeat the sequence starting with the next push-up.

exercises.name: Push up to Down Dog
exercises.description:
Perform a regular push-up, but after each push-up, flow into downward dog, then back to plank. 

This targets the chest, shoulders, and core while improving flexibility.

exercises.name: Push Up to T-rotation
exercises.alternateName: bodyweight push up to thoracic opener
exercises.description: Perform a push-up, then rotate your body into a T position, extending one arm to the ceiling. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Push-Up to Toe Reach
exercises.description: From a push-up position, lower your chest toward the floor, then press up and reach one hand toward the opposite toe before returning to start and alternating sides.

exercises.name: QUAD BLASTER: Landmine Front Squat Purmotion Wishbone Attachment
exercises.description: QUAD BLASTER: Landmine Front Squat Purmotion Wishbone Attachment

exercises.name: Quadruped Hip Abduction Holds
exercises.description: In a quadruped position, lift one knee outward and hold the position to activate the glutes and outer hips.
